descriptions: Abete clan was included in the Scissionisti alliance.

descriptions: Midden Noord is a rival of AFCA.

descriptions: F-side lists Midden Noord as rivals.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Midden Noord

descriptions: After the Golden Dragon massacre, Wah Ching drove the Hop Sing out of San Francisco.
edges.evidence_quote: In the wake of the 1977 shooting, the Wah Ching were ascendant and the Hop Sing were chased out of San Francisco.

descriptions: Hop Sing Boys is listed as a rival in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Wah Ching , Hop Sing Boys

descriptions: The Hop Sing and Wo Hop To merged under an alliance led by Peter Chong and Raymond Chow.
edges.evidence_quote: Under the alliance, the Hop Sing and Wo Hop To merged, and an umbrella organization, Tien Hu Wui, was formed to oversee their combined business.

descriptions: The text mentions both organizations in the context of Chow's ties, but does not assert a direct relationship between them.
edges.evidence_quote: ties to a San Francisco Chinatown street gang and an organized crime syndicate, including the American branch of the Hong Kong -based triad Wo Hop To and the Hop Sing Boys.

Abu Sayyaf.description: Jihadist militant and pirate group based in the Philippines, founded in 1991 by Abdurajak Abubakar Janjalani. It follows Wahhabi Sunni Islam and has been responsible for bombings, kidnappings, and assassinations. The group aligned with the Islamic State in 2014. It was declared dismantled in 2024 but resurfaced in 2026.

descriptions: 14K cooperates with Abu Sayyaf in arms smuggling and money laundering.
edges.evidence_quote: The 14K triad has been involved in smuggling arms to Abu Sayyaf and has reportedly cooperated with the Islamic group in laundering and transmitting ransom money

descriptions: 14K is listed as an ally of Abu Sayyaf in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: 14K, Maute Group, Al-Qaeda (formerly)

descriptions: Abu Sayyaf is allied with 14K.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: 14K , Maute Group , Al-Qaeda (formerly)

descriptions: Abu Sayyaf coordinated with 14K Triad for kidnappings and received military support.
edges.evidence_quote: Abu Sayyaf coordinated with the Chinese 14K Triad gang in carrying out the kidnappings. The 14K Triad has militarily supported Abu Sayyaf.

descriptions: 14K is listed as an ally of Abu Sayyaf.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: 14K

descriptions: Al-Qaeda provided initial funding to Abu Sayyaf.
edges.evidence_quote: The government noted that initial funding for ASG came from al-Qaeda through the brother-in-law of Osama bin Laden, Mohammed Jamal Khalifa.

descriptions: Abu Sayyaf was formerly allied with Al-Qaeda.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: 14K , Maute Group , Al-Qaeda (formerly)

descriptions: Al-Qaeda is listed as a former ally of Abu Sayyaf.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: 14K , Maute Group , Al-Qaeda (formerly)

descriptions: Al-Qaeda was formerly an ally of Abu Sayyaf.

descriptions: Barahama Alih Group is alleged to be responsible for kidnappings, but no direct relationship with Abu Sayyaf is stated; they are separate groups.
edges.evidence_quote: Military sources allege Barahama Alih Group was responsible for a number of kidnapping incidents in previous years including the abduction of Australian national Warren Rodwell, Italian priest Giancarlo Bossi, and some local Filipinos.

descriptions: Daulat Ul-Islamiya is allied to Abu Sayyaf.
edges.evidence_quote: saying a group allied to them; the Daulat Ul-Islamiya were responsible.

descriptions: Abu Sayyaf pledged allegiance to Islamic State, becoming its East Asia Province.
edges.evidence_quote: On July 23, 2014, Isnilon Hapilon, one of the group's leaders, swore an oath of loyalty to Abu Bakr al-Baghdadi, the leader of the Islamic State (IS) officially aligning the group with the global jihadist movement.

descriptions: Abu Sayyaf is a part of the Islamic State.

descriptions: Abu Sayyaf fought alongside the Maute Group in the Battle of Marawi.
edges.evidence_quote: In May 2017, Hapilon and other members of Abu Sayyaf joined the Islamic extremist Maute Group based in Lanao del Sur during their attempt to seize control of Marawi City.

edges.evidence_quote: Abdurajak Abubakar Janjalani's first recruits were soldiers of the MNLF and the Moro Islamic Liberation Front (MILF).

edges.evidence_quote: The Ajang Ajang group ( Tausug : Baanan Ajang Ajang , Sulat Sūg : بَأَنَنْ اَجَڠْ٢) was a cell within the terrorist organization Abu Sayyaf .

descriptions: Abu Sayyaf was a former affiliate of al-Qaeda until it pledged allegiance to ISIL.

Dödspatrullen.description: Swedish Somali organized crime syndicate originating from Rinkeby, Stockholm, founded in 2015. Engaged in illegal drug trade, murders, and other crimes, operating in Sweden, Finland, Denmark, Morocco, and Spain. Known for a long-running gang war with rival Somali group Shottaz since 2015, resulting in multiple deaths.

descriptions: Dödspatrullen and Shottaz have been in a gang war since 2015, resulting in multiple deaths.
edges.evidence_quote: Dödspatrullen has been in a gang war with Shottaz, another Swedish Somali criminal organization originating from Rinkeby, since 2015.

descriptions: National Islamic Front is an offshoot of the Muslim Brotherhood in Sudan.
edges.evidence_quote: An offshoot of the Sudanese branch of the Muslim Brotherhood, the Islamic Charter Front grew during the 1960

descriptions: Finnish Resistance Movement cooperated with Feuerkrieg Division.
edges.evidence_quote: The Finnish Resistance Movement and its successor Atomwaffen Finland cooperated with the group as well.

descriptions: De Bernardo group is a satellite group operating under Mazzarella clan's command.
edges.evidence_quote: The De Bernardo group (led by Rosario De Bernardo, brother of Vincenzo "o pisello", murdered in 2015) handled large-scale distribution of cocaine, crack, marijuana, and hashish, primarily in Somma Vesuviana.

On Leong Chinese Merchants Association.description: A tong society based in Chinatown, Manhattan, established in November 1893. It fought violent wars with the Hip Sing Tong for control of Chinatown's rackets and businesses. In recent years, linked to the Ghost Shadows street gang. Currently has over 30,000 registered members, mostly with commercial or industrial backgrounds.

descriptions: Ghost Shadows was affiliated with On Leong Tong.
edges.evidence_quote: the gang was affiliated with the On Leong Tong

descriptions: Hip Sing Tong and On Leong Tong were rivals engaged in violent Tong wars.
edges.evidence_quote: The Hip Sing Tong, along with their rivals the Four Brothers and the On Leong Tong , would be involved in violent Tong wars for control of Chinatown during the early 1900s.

descriptions: Hip Sing Tong and On Leong Tong fought in the Tong Wars.
edges.evidence_quote: This became known as the "Tong Wars", which were a series of violent attacks between two branches of the Tong Gang, the Hip Sing Tong and On Leong Tong.

descriptions: On Leong has been linked to the Ghost Shadows street gang.
edges.evidence_quote: In recent years, the Tong has been linked to the Ghost Shadows street gang led by Wing Yeung Chan

descriptions: On Leong and Hip Sing Tong fought a violent war for control of Chinatown's rackets and businesses.
edges.evidence_quote: the tong fought a violent war for control of Chinatown's rackets and businesses with the Hip Sing Tong

descriptions: Tse briefly joined On Leong but was expelled, indicating a conflict or falling out.
edges.evidence_quote: Tse joined the On Leong, but they expelled him shortly afterward

Primeiro Grupo Catarinense.description: The largest criminal organization in the Brazilian state of Santa Catarina, founded on March 3, 2003, in the Florianópolis penitentiary. It commands rebellions, robberies, kidnappings, murders, and drug trafficking, operating mainly in Santa Catarina with connections in at least three other Brazilian states and borders with Paraguay, Bolivia, and Peru. The group has 14 leaders divided into two 'ministries' and is modeled after the Comando Vermelho and Primeiro Comando da Capital.

descriptions: Primeiro Grupo Catarinense is listed as an ally of Bala na Cara in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Aliados: Comando Vermelho , Primeiro Grupo Catarinense , Os Aberto

edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Primeiro Grupo Catarinense, Bala na Cara

descriptions: PGC allied with Comando Vermelho for information exchange and training in weapons handling and crime sophistication.
edges.evidence_quote: A principal delas foi com o Comando Vermelho , liderado pelo narcotraficante Fernandinho Beira-Mar , a quem coube, além de trocas de informações, o aperfeiçoamento dos recrutados pelo PGC no manuseio de armas e de fuzis e a sofisticação no modus operandi dos crimes.

descriptions: The Corporation is listed as an ally of the Purple Gang in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Five Families (especially the 116th Street Crew of the Genovese crime family), The Council, The Corporation, and various Latin American drug trafficking organizations and African American organized crime groups

edges.evidence_quote: Allies: American Mafia , Colombian Cartels, Mexican Cartels, Cuban mafia

descriptions: The Corporation had violent turf wars with various Italian mafia families.

Harlem Spartans.description: British hip-hop collective based in Kennington, London, formed in 2014. Known for pioneering UK drill music, with members amassing over 100 million streams. Police allege it is a gang, which the group denies. Has a notable rivalry with Brixton-based group 410.

descriptions: Harlem Spartans has a rivalry with 410, expressed through diss tracks and physical violence.
edges.evidence_quote: The group is known for its rivalry with Brixton -based group 410.

descriptions: Harlem Spartans is affiliated with Moscow17.
edges.evidence_quote: Harlem Spartans is affiliated with Walworth -based group Moscow17.

descriptions: Onefour cites Harlem Spartans as a major influence.
edges.evidence_quote: Australian drill group Onefour cites Harlem Spartans as a major influence in their music.

descriptions: In Gangs of London, Albanian mafia boss Luan Dushaj is an associate of the Wallace Organisation.
edges.evidence_quote: One of the main crime bosses in Gangs of London is Luan Dushaj ... the head of the powerful Albanian mafia in London ... one of the associates of Wallace family

Charlestown Gang.description: Irish American organized crime gang based in Charlestown, Boston, Massachusetts, founded in the 1950s by the McLaughlin brothers. The gang controlled gambling and loan sharking on the docks and carried out contract killings for the Patriarca and Genovese crime families. They were involved in a violent gang war with the Winter Hill Gang from 1961 until their demise in 1966.

descriptions: The Charlestown Gang performed contract killings for the Genovese crime family.
edges.evidence_quote: The McLaughlin brothers forged ties with the Mafia, carrying out contract killings for the Patriarca crime family in Boston and the Genovese crime family in New York City.

descriptions: The Charlestown Gang performed contract killings for the Patriarca crime family.
edges.evidence_quote: The McLaughlin brothers forged ties with the Mafia, carrying out contract killings for the Patriarca crime family in Boston and the Genovese crime family in New York City.

descriptions: The two gangs were engaged in a violent gang war from 1961 onward.
edges.evidence_quote: The Charlestown Gang became involved in a gang war with a rival Irish mob group, the Winter Hill Gang of Somerville led by James 'Buddy' McLean, beginning in 1961.

descriptions: Infobox lists Charlestown Gang as rival.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Charlestown Gang, Gustin Gang, Winter Hill Gang, and various other gangs in New England

edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Charlestown Gang, Gustin Gang, Winter Hill Gang, and various other gangs in New England

edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Charlestown Gang

edges.evidence_quote: After vanquishing the McLaughlin Gang of Charlestown

edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Charlestown Gang

descriptions: The East Side Gang and the River Gang shared bootlegging rackets and other underworld activities.
edges.evidence_quote: Milazzo's Mafia group and the Eastside Gang maintained alliances with other mob factions, including the River Gang, led by brothers Thomas and Peter Licavoli who shared the local bootlegging rackets and various underworld activities with each other.

descriptions: The East Side Gang and the Westside Mob fought in the Crosstown Mafia War.
edges.evidence_quote: The Fish Market Murders developed into the major gang conflict between the Eastside and Westside Mobs, which would later be known as the Crosstown Mafia War.

descriptions: LaMare's Westside Mob attempted to assassinate Eastside leaders, escalating the Crosstown War.
edges.evidence_quote: LaMare scheduled a meeting between the two divisions of Detroit at a Vernor Highway fish market, where he planned for three gunman to assassinate Eastside mob bosses.

Four Seas Gang.description: The Four Seas Gang is a triad society based in Taiwan, founded in May 1954 by mainland Chinese and their descendants who fled with the KMT. It has an estimated membership of 10,000 in Taiwan and is active in alien smuggling in Southern California, with territories in Los Angeles and Santa Ana. The gang uses grey as its color and has 86 branches with up to 50,000 members and partners.

descriptions: Four Seas Gang is a triad based in Taiwan.
edges.evidence_quote: Based elsewhere: - Four Seas Gang , Taiwan

descriptions: The Order II grew out of the original Order, attempting to perpetuate its activities.
edges.evidence_quote: Order II (Bruder Schweigen Strike Force II) was also anti-government and antisemitic.

67.description: 67 is a British music group and street gang in the UK drill scene, originally from Brixton Hill, South London. Formed in 2012, they are known as early adopters of the UK drill sound and gained notoriety after the police shooting of member Chris Kaba. The Metropolitan Police has labelled them a criminal gang, and they have faced restrictions on performances. They are still active as of the present.

descriptions: 150 had a rivalry with 67.
edges.evidence_quote: 150 would develop a rivalry with Brixton Hill -based group 67

descriptions: 67 member killed a member of 150 in a gang dispute.
edges.evidence_quote: On 25 February 2015, a member of the drill group and street gang, 150, Dwayne Simpson aka SQ, was stabbed to death by a member of 67 over a gang dispute.

descriptions: 67 member killed Cheyon Evans, who was allegedly part of 37/OJB, a rival gang.
edges.evidence_quote: Cheyon Evans was known as 'Stompz' and allegedly was a part of the 37/OJB gang, with him also having ties to the CT gang based in Clapham, one of 67's main rivals.

descriptions: CT is described as one of 67's main rivals.
edges.evidence_quote: Cheyon Evans was known as 'Stompz' and allegedly was a part of the 37/OJB gang, with him also having ties to the CT gang based in Clapham, one of 67's main rivals.

descriptions: Bandokay cited 67 as an influence.
edges.evidence_quote: Bandokay credited fellow OFB rapper Headie One as a major influence, as well as UK drill group 67 and American rapper Lil Durk.

Carecas do ABC.description: A skinhead gang dissident from Carecas do Subúrbio, formed in the Greater ABC region of Brazil, primarily in Santo André. Currently present in São Paulo and other Brazilian capitals. The group is territorial, moderately politicized, anticommunist, and antiliberal, advocating violence against perceived 'rotten sectors of society'. It admits black and northeastern members, distinguishing it from other skinhead groups.

descriptions: Carecas do ABC is listed as an ally of Brazilian militias in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Cosa Nostra, Clan del Golfo, Group America, Carecas do ABC, Carecas do Subúrbio, Police Death squad, Neo-integralist groups, Bicheiros, Diehard Duterte Supporters

descriptions: Carecas do ABC split from Carecas do Subúrbio as a dissident faction.
edges.evidence_quote: Carecas do ABC emerged in 1987 as a dissident from the group Carecas do Subúrbio.

descriptions: Carecas do ABC is a dissident faction that split from Carecas do Subúrbio.
edges.evidence_quote: Carecas do ABC são uma gangue skinhead dissidente do grupo Carecas do Subúrbio

descriptions: Sombra Negra considered FMLN an enemy during the civil war.
edges.evidence_quote: Enemigos: FMLN ( Durante la Guerra Civil )

'Ndrina Lo Presti.description: The Lo Presti 'ndrina is a Mafia clan of the 'Ndrangheta, originally from Marina di Gioiosa Jonica in Calabria, Italy, and based in Bardonecchia, Piedmont since the late 1950s. It is known as the Maneja family in Calabria and is closely related to the Mazzaferro clan. The family became dominant in the Val di Susa area and was involved in drug and arms trafficking, extortion, usury, and infiltration of public administration. In 1995, the President of Italy dissolved the Bardonecchia city council due to mafia infiltration, the first such case in northern Italy.

descriptions: Lo Presti had ties with Alvaro.
edges.evidence_quote: Ha avuto legami con tutte le più importanti famiglie mafiose della Calabria e della Sicilia . I Belfiore , i Tripodo , i Mesiani Mazzacuva, i Ruga , gli Aquino , gli Ierinò , i Macrì , i Morabito , i Varacalli , i Cataldo , gli Strangio , i Barbaro , gli Alvaro , i Palamara , i Raso-Albanese e i Facchineri .

edges.evidence_quote: Luciano Ursino, entra in rapporti di affari con i fratelli Adolfo e Aldo Cosimo Crea, originari di Stilo , in Calabria , boss emergenti della 'Ndrangheta torinese.

Africa Corps.description: Russian expeditionary paramilitary group controlled by the Russian government, established in 2023 after the death of Wagner Group leadership. It subsumed Wagner's operations in Africa to support Russian influence and allied governments. Operates in Mali, Burkina Faso, Niger, Libya, Central African Republic, and also deployed in Ukraine. Focuses on military support, training, and counterterrorism.

descriptions: Azawad Liberation Front shot down an Africa Corps helicopter, indicating conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: a helicopter belonging to the corps was shot down by the Azawad Liberation Front

descriptions: Africa Corps cooperates with Bears Brigade in Africa.
edges.evidence_quote: The formation cooperates with other government-controlled structures, like the "Bears Brigade" in Africa.

descriptions: Africa Corps rescued hostages from Jama'at Nusrat al-Islam wal-Muslimin, indicating conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: The corps rescued two employees of a Russian geological exploration company who were abducted by Jama'at Nusrat al-Islam wal-Muslimin in Niger two years earlier.

descriptions: Redut's leader is involved in the creation and operations of Africa Corps.
edges.evidence_quote: Konstantin Mirzayants, a purported leader of Redut, is reportedly closely involved in the MoD's operations in Africa and the creation of the Africa Corps.

descriptions: Africa Corps subsumed and rebranded Wagner Group's operations in Africa.
edges.evidence_quote: The Corps largely took over the operations of the Wagner Group in Africa, by subsuming and rebranding its structures.

descriptions: Africa Corps was a rival to Wagner Group and later absorbed its Africa operations.
edges.evidence_quote: a US government source said that the Africa Corps was a rival to Wagner that aimed to absorb its personnel and activities in Africa.

descriptions: 'Ndrina Ferrazzo made agreements with clans from Crotone and Lombardy.
edges.evidence_quote: sarebbero stati realizzati accordi con clan del Crotonese e della Lombardia

Almighty Insane Popes Nation.description: Chicago street gang formed in the late 1950s on the north side, originally from a Greek greaser gang. The name originally stood for 'Protecting Our People Eliminating Spics' but later changed to 'scum'. The gang split into north and south factions, with the north side allied with Folk Nation and the south side with People Nation. Active in Chicago with rivalries against Latin Kings and other gangs.

descriptions: Insane North Side Popes are listed as rivals of the Gaylords in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Folk Nation , Insane Deuces, Simon City Royals , Insane Spanish Cobras , Maniac Latin Disciples , C-Notes, Insane North Side Popes

descriptions: The north side Popes rival the Black P. Stones per infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: People Nation , Insane Deuces, Familia Stones, Almighty Gaylords , Latin Kings , Black P. Stones , Assyrian Kings

descriptions: The Gaylords killed a Popes leader, indicating conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: In 1975, Larkin was killed by members of the Almighty Gaylords

Aggi Crew.description: A criminal drug gang based in the St Paul's district of Bristol, England, active in the 1990s. The name derives from 'aggravated burglary'. They dominated Bristol's drug market until 1998 when six members were jailed. After release, they attempted to reclaim territory from Yardie gangs, leading to a turf war involving shootings and armed police patrols.

descriptions: Aggi Crew and Hype Crew were in conflict over territory, leading to a turf war.
edges.evidence_quote: The power vacuum that this created was quickly filled by Yardie gangs such as the Hype Crew and Mountain View Posse.

descriptions: Aggi Crew and Mountain View Posse were in conflict over territory, leading to a turf war.
edges.evidence_quote: The power vacuum that this created was quickly filled by Yardie gangs such as the Hype Crew and Mountain View Posse.

Al-Qaeda in the Arabian Peninsula.description: Sunni Islamist militant organization based in Yemen, formed in 2009 from the merger of al-Qaeda in Yemen and al-Qaeda in Saudi Arabia. It seeks to overthrow the Yemeni government and establish an Islamic emirate. AQAP is considered the most active al-Qaeda affiliate and has conducted high-profile attacks in the West, including the 2009 Northwest Airlines Flight 253 bombing attempt and the 2015 Charlie Hebdo shooting. Its strength has waned due to internal struggles, civil war, and US drone strikes.
